extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Two rules write to distinct outputs so they cannot conflict via priority: rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) sets rec_drug_a:=true when (polypharmacy AND low_ejection_fraction AND ckd_dialysis AND hypertension), and rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b:=true when polypharmacy. The conjunction that triggers rec_drug_a necessarily includes polypharmacy=true, which simultaneously triggers rec_drug_b. With polypharmacy=true, low_ejection_fraction=true, ckd_dialysis=true, hypertension=true: rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true. No rule ever sets either output to false, and the default false only applies to outputs left unset (both are set here), so neither value is overridden. Both outputs are jointly true, violating mutual exclusion. The remaining inputs (g6pd_deficiency, peptic_ulcer, heart_failure) only affect rec_drug_c and are set to false; they do not impact the violation.
